Tests if dividing `Number.NaN` by `-0` results in `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of dividing `Number.NaN` by negative zero in JavaScript. The `assert` function verifies that the result of this operation is `NaN`, which is expected to evaluate to `true` when passed to the `isNaN` function. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles this specific arithmetic operation according to the ECMAScript specification. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a single mathematical operation and its expected outcome.
